title: Sensing materials, method for making functional devices and applications thereof
abstract: The present invention relates to a functional device having a functional sensor comprising of a sensor part and interdigitated electrode arrays, wherein the sensor part comprises a sensing material and, the interdigitated electrode arrays are printed electrodes. Further the present invention relates to a method of making the functional device.
claims:
Claims Claims A functional device having a functional sensor comprising of a sensor part and interdigitated electrode arrays, wherein the sensor part comprises a sensing material and, the interdigitated electrode arrays are printed electrodes. The functional device of claim 1 , wherein the sensing material is a composite comprising two or more materials wherein one of the materials is optionally an additive, and the other material/materials is/are selected from the group consisting of non-conducting material, semi-conducting material, and conducting material. The functional device of any one of claims 1 -2, wherein the nonconducting material is a synthetic amorphous silica, preferably selected from the group consisting of fumed silica, precipitated porous silica, precipitated non-porous silica and silica aerogel, wherein the non-conducting material is also synthetic titanium dioxide, preferably selected from the group consisting of precipitated porous titanium dioxide, and precipitated non-porous titanium dioxide and/or wherein, the conducting material selected from the group consisting of carbon and carbon based materials, preferably where in the carbon and carbon based materials are selected from the group consisting of carbon dots, carbon nanoparticles, carbon sheets, carbon nanotubes, and graphenes and/or wherein the semi-conducting material is a metalloporphyrin dye; preferably the metalloporphyrin dye comprise at least one metal ion selected from the group consisting of Sc 3+ , Zr + , Lu 3+ , Cr 3+ , Mn 3+ , Fe 3+ , Co 3+ , Mo 5+ , Ru 2+ , and Mg 2+ ; and preferably the metalloporphyrin dye comprise alkyl or aryl groups with R a minimum of 4 and/or wherein the semi-conducting material is a composite of silica, or silicon, and carbon; wherein the molar ratios of composite between Si to C varies between 0.01 to 1 , preferably between 0.2 to 0.6. 

 The functional device of claim 2, wherein said additive is a binder; preferably one selected from the group consisting of nitrocellulose, polyurethan resins, polyvinylbutyral, PVC-copolymers, ketonic resins, fumaric & maleic resins, polyamides, ethylcellulose, and acrylic resins. The functional device of any one of claims 1 -4, wherein the printed electrodes are arranged at a distance, wherein the distance may vary between 100 nm to 1000 pm, preferably between 100 nm to 500 pm; preferably between 5 pm to 200 pm; preferably the printed electrodes may be screen printed or lithographic printed or ink jet printed electrodes. The functional device of any one of claims 1 -5, where in the functional device may be integrated with electronic components, preferably wherein the electronic components are passive, active or battery- assisted passive tags, preferably wherein the said components frequency range between 9 KHz to 3000 GHz, preferably between 13.56 MHz to 960 MHz and/or where in the functional device may be integrated with electronic components, preferably a circuit, where in the circuit comprises of two or more transistors. Method of making a functional device according to anyone of claim 1 - 6, wherein the method comprises: a) adding a sensing material to solvents capable of forming a slurry by mechanical agitation; b) applying the slurry of a) onto a surface of at least one interdigitated electrode array to form a fine layer of coating; c) subjecting the applied fine layer of coating of b) to thermal treatment and thereby obtaining a surface coated interdigitated electrode arrays; d) integrating the surface coated interdigitated electrode arrays of c) with electronic components, and thereby obtaining fully functional sensor with a gas sensing function. 8. The method according to claim 7, wherein the solvent is a non-polar solvent, selected from the group consisting of toluene, n-hexane, dichloromethane, pyridine, chloroform, and methyl ethyl ketone and/or wherein the solvent is a conductive ink, preferably selected from the group consisting of UV curable inks, conductive inks and semiconducting polymers. 9. The method according to claim 7-8, wherein the slurry of step a) comprise sensing material of at least 1 and at most 99 %, preferably between 5 to 70%. 10. The method according to claim 7-9, wherein the slurry of step 9a) is used to print interdigitated electrode arrays by screen-printing method, wherein the array distance may vary between 100 nm to 1000 pm, preferably between 100 pm to 500 pm.
